turns.content: George Papadopoulos had his fasting blood test this morning. Now I need to set up the rest of his diabetes cycle — results review next week and then his medication review two weeks after that. All with Dr Chen.
Let me get George's details and set up the full quarterly cycle.

result.notes: Type 2 diabetes — quarterly cycle: fasting blood test (Mon/Tue morning, must be fasting) → results review with Dr Chen 1 week later → dietitian referral if HbA1c elevated. On metformin 1000mg twice daily. Wife Maria manages his diet and usually calls with questions about results.

turns.content: Good, 10am Monday for his results review. Now the medication review — that should be about 2 weeks after.
Checking Dr Chen's availability for the week of March 31.
Perfect — blood test today, results review on the 17th, medication review on the 31st. That's his full quarterly cycle sorted. His wife Maria might call about the results too — she usually does.
All three stages of George's quarterly cycle are now booked. I'll note that Maria may call about results.
This is the same pattern every 3 months — blood test, then results a week later, then medication review two weeks after. Like clockwork.
Ben O'Sullivan is due for his asthma cycle too. He needs a spirometry test first, then a review with Dr Chen a week later. Can you set both up?
Let me find slots for both appointments in Ben's 3-monthly cycle.

result.notes: Asthma — 3-monthly cycle: spirometry test → review with Dr Chen (1 week later) → adjust preventer if needed. Also gets annual flu vaccine in March. Action plan on file — step-up protocol if peak flow drops below 80%.

turns.content: Spirometry on the 18th at 9am. Now the follow-up review — try the following week.
Checking Dr Chen's availability for the week after.
Sorted. Spirometry the 18th, review the 25th. Same deal as George — it's the same pattern every quarter for these chronic patients. Test first, then review, then adjust meds if needed.

Angela Romano called — she had her blood test last Friday and needs to book her GP follow-up with Dr Chen. Her rheumatologist already reviewed the results. She also mentioned her hands are flaring up again.
Let me look up Angela's details and find a slot for her GP follow-up.

result.notes: Rheumatoid arthritis — 6-weekly cycle: blood test (CRP + ESR + liver function) → rheumatologist review (external, Dr Webb at Westmead) → GP follow-up with Dr Chen 2 weeks later to adjust methotrexate if needed. Flares up in winter — may need extra urgent appointments May-August. Keep Friday afternoons free for her when possible.

turns.content: Friday afternoon is best for her — try 2pm. And if she's flaring up, Dr Chen might want to see her sooner for the methotrexate adjustment.
Booking Angela with Dr Chen at 2pm Friday for her cycle follow-up.

params.message: Hi Angela, your GP follow-up with Dr Chen has been booked for Friday 21 March at 2:00pm. Please bring your latest rheumatologist notes from Dr Webb. Call (02) 9555 0100 if you need to change this.

turns.content: Her cycle is every 6 weeks — blood test, then rheumatologist externally, then back to us for the GP review. It's like a three-step dance every time. And she always needs Fridays.
